title: Crocodilodes eriobasis
intro:
I couldn't find any information on a plant called Crocodilodes eriobasis. However, I found a plant called Croton eriobasis, also known as Crocodilodes eriobasis in some sources. 

Here is a paragraph with the given information about Croton eriobasis:

Croton eriobasis is a species of flowering plant in the spurge family (Euphorbiaceae), native to tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Croton eriobasis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Croton eriobasis is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of conditions, including fever, colds, and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Crocodilodes eriobasis belongs to the family Hesperiidae, which is a group of butterflies commonly known as skippers. The genus Crocodilodes is a part of this family and is found in the tropical regions of Southeast Asia. The classification of Crocodilodes eriobasis is further divided into the order Lepidoptera, which includes butterflies and moths. This species is a member of the subfamily Pyrginae, a subfamily of skippers characterized by their distinctive markings. By understanding the classification of Crocodilodes eriobasis, we can better appreciate the diversity and complexity of the natural world.
